purpose: Coverage planning for broadly useful human-agent collaboration Skills. This is not approval, release inventory, or runtime installation evidence.
principles: Cover common digital collaboration contexts beyond software engineering.
Separate general-purpose Skills from specialist domain Skills.
Prefer reusable collaboration patterns over narrow tool wrappers unless the tool is a durable work surface.
High-risk professional domains require conservative framing, source evidence, and human or domain review.
Runtime-native, official, or platform-owned capabilities remain baselines or adapters unless separately curated.

skillTypes.name: General collaboration Skill
skillTypes.definition: Reusable across many domains, roles, and tools with minimal domain-specific dependency.

skillTypes.name: Specialist collaboration Skill
skillTypes.definition: Useful for a durable domain, profession, toolchain, or workflow, with explicit dependencies and boundaries.

domains.label: Daily life and personal productivity
domains.coverageGoal: Help users organize personal tasks, decisions, notes, plans, routines, and lightweight life-admin work without overclaiming professional advice.
riskNotes: Avoid medical, legal, financial, or safety-critical advice without domain boundaries.

domains.label: Office and knowledge work
domains.coverageGoal: Cover documents, spreadsheets, presentations, email, meetings, summaries, reports, and cross-tool office workflows.
riskNotes: Protect confidential business data and account-bound tool dependencies.

domains.label: Software engineering
domains.coverageGoal: Keep strong coverage for requirements, architecture, implementation, testing, review, CI/CD, security, observability, release, migration, and maintenance.
riskNotes: Do not duplicate runtime-native capabilities without a clear cross-agent portability gain.

domains.label: Business and commercial work
domains.coverageGoal: Support product, marketing, sales, customer success, operations, strategy, competitive research, and launch planning.
riskNotes: Separate evidence-backed business analysis from speculative claims or regulated advice.

domains.label: Education and training
domains.coverageGoal: Support lesson planning, tutoring, practice, feedback, assessment design, and skill development across subjects.
riskNotes: Avoid replacing qualified instruction for regulated or safety-critical training.

domains.label: Academic and research work
domains.coverageGoal: Support literature review, citation-aware synthesis, experiment planning, data interpretation, paper drafting, peer review, and reproducibility.
riskNotes: Require source traceability, citation discipline, and explicit uncertainty handling.

domains.label: Creative production and design
domains.coverageGoal: Support design ideation, visual production planning, writing, story shaping, prompts, brand work, and creative review.
riskNotes: Respect asset licensing, attribution, and platform/tool-specific dependencies.

domains.label: Data analytics and reporting
domains.coverageGoal: Support data-quality checks, KPI design, dashboards, reports, exploratory analysis, and decision support.
riskNotes: Preserve calculation provenance, sample limitations, and privacy boundaries.

domains.label: Operations, projects, and process
domains.coverageGoal: Support project planning, status reporting, risk management, process improvement, retrospectives, and operational handoffs.
riskNotes: Keep recommendations contextual and avoid hiding unresolved authority or accountability decisions.

domains.label: Security, privacy, and compliance
domains.coverageGoal: Support security review, privacy checks, compliance-aware documentation, threat modeling, and risk triage.
riskNotes: High-risk findings require evidence, severity discipline, and human review before operational decisions.
